title: B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives
content: The B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science program with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives provides a robust education in forensic science, with a focus on the analysis of toxic substances and explosive materials. This program prepares students to analyze toxic substances, investigate poisoning cases, and understand the dynamics and investigation of explosive incidents, equipping them for careers in forensic laboratories, law enforcement, and related fields. The curriculum integrates core forensic science subjects with specialized courses in forensic toxicology and explosives, enhanced by practical training, laboratory work, internships, and field experiences. Graduates of the B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Toxicology and Explosives have a wide range of career opportunities, including: Forensic Toxicologist, Explosives Specialist, Crime Scene Investigator, Environmental and Occupational Toxicologist. XIIth with Science with minimum 50% marks Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 01 Core Chemical Forensics Foundation: Provide rigorous training in analytical chemistry techniques (spectroscopy, chromatography, mass spectrometry) applied to forensic evidence analysis including drugs, poisons, explosives and trace materials. 02 Toxicology Specialization: Develop expertise in postmortem toxicology, drug metabolism, poison detection and interpretation of toxicological findings in legal contexts using modern laboratory instrumentation. 03 Forensic Evidence Processing: Train students in standard operating procedures for chemical evidence collection, preservation, and analysis while maintaining chain-of-custody protocols for courtroom admissibility. 04 Research & Method Development: Cultivate research skills through projects on novel detection methods, designer drug analysis, environmental toxicology and emerging challenges in chemical forensics. 05 Industry & Regulatory Preparedness : Prepare graduates for careers in crime labs, medical examiner offices, pharmaceutical companies and regulatory agencies through internships and training aligned with SWGDRUG and SOFT/AAFS standards. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 02 03
